# DeutschMeister

> A free German language learning platform covering CEFR levels A1.1 to B2.2.

## About

DeutschMeister (https://deutsch-meister.de) is a comprehensive German grammar and vocabulary learning resource. All content is free to access without an account.

## What We Offer

### Grammar (64 Topics)
- 8 CEFR sub-levels: A1.1, A1.2, A2.1, A2.2, B1.1, B1.2, B2.1, B2.2
- 8 grammar topics per level
- Every topic explained in English first, then German
- Interactive exercises with answer explanations
- Audio for all examples
- Topics include: cases (nominative, accusative, dative, genitive), articles, prepositions, verb conjugation, modal verbs, separable verbs, word order, sentence structure, and more

### Vocabulary
- Organized by CEFR level
- Words with translations, examples, and audio

### Sentences
- Example sentences for each level
- Translations and audio included

### Listening
- Listening exercises for each level
- Audio with comprehension questions

### Podcasts
- Video podcasts for listening practice
- Levels A1.1 to A2.1 available
- Native German speech with level-appropriate content

## Complete Grammar Topic List

### A1.1 - Complete Beginner
1. Alphabet & Pronunciation
2. Nouns & Gender
3. Definite Articles
4. Indefinite Articles
5. Personal Pronouns
6. Verb sein
7. Verb haben
8. Present Tense Regular

### A1.2 - Elementary
1. Basic Sentence Structure
2. Nominative Case
3. Accusative Case Introduction
4. Numbers & Counting
5. Question Words
6. Negation
7. Modal Verbs Introduction
8. Prepositions with Accusative

### A2.1 - Pre-Intermediate
1. Dative Case
2. Prepositions with Dative
3. Two-Way Prepositions
4. Possessive Pronouns
5. Separable Verbs
6. Perfect Tense with haben
7. Perfect Tense with sein
8. Imperative Mood

### A2.2 - Lower Intermediate
1. Reflexive Verbs
2. Simple Past sein haben
3. Coordinating Conjunctions
4. Subordinating Conjunctions
5. Word Order in Subordinate Clauses
6. Comparative
7. Superlative
8. Future Tense

### B1.1 - Intermediate
1. Genitive Case
2. Genitive Prepositions
3. Relative Clauses Nominative Accusative
4. Relative Clauses Dative Genitive
5. Konjunktiv II würde
6. Konjunktiv II wäre hätte
7. Infinitive with zu
8. um zu ohne zu

### B1.2 - Upper Intermediate
1. Passive Voice Present
2. Passive Voice Past
3. Verbs with Prepositions
4. Adjective Declension Strong
5. Adjective Declension Weak Mixed
6. Simple Past Narrative
7. Indirect Questions
8. N-Declension Nouns

### B2.1 - Advanced
1. Konjunktiv II Past
2. Konjunktiv I Reported Speech
3. Passive Alternatives
4. Participial Adjectives
5. Extended Attributes
6. Double Infinitive
7. Causative Constructions
8. Nominalization

### B2.2 - Upper Advanced
1. Advanced Conjunctions
2. Modal Particles
3. Functional Verb Structures
4. Subjunctive Fixed Expressions
5. Complex Sentence Building
6. Register and Style
7. Future Perfect
8. Review and Integration

## Key Features

- Bilingual toggle (English/German interface)
- No account required for free content
- Mobile-friendly design
- Audio pronunciation for vocabulary and examples
- Exercises with explanations for why answers are correct

## Target Audience

- English speakers learning German
- Self-study learners
- Beginner to upper-intermediate levels (A1-B2)
- Anyone preparing for German language exams

## URL Structure

Individual grammar topics are available at:
https://deutsch-meister.de/grammar/{level}/{topic-slug}

Examples:
- https://deutsch-meister.de/grammar/a1.1/alphabet-pronunciation
- https://deutsch-meister.de/grammar/a1.2/accusative-intro
- https://deutsch-meister.de/grammar/a2.1/dative-case
- https://deutsch-meister.de/grammar/b1.1/genitive-case
- https://deutsch-meister.de/grammar/b2.1/konjunktiv-i-reported-speech
- https://deutsch-meister.de/grammar/b2.2/modal-particles

Grammar level pages:
- https://deutsch-meister.de/grammar/a1.1
- https://deutsch-meister.de/grammar/a1.2
- https://deutsch-meister.de/grammar/a2.1
- https://deutsch-meister.de/grammar/a2.2
- https://deutsch-meister.de/grammar/b1.1
- https://deutsch-meister.de/grammar/b1.2
- https://deutsch-meister.de/grammar/b2.1
- https://deutsch-meister.de/grammar/b2.2
